order-by.label: ORDER BY Clause
order-by.summary: Sort query results by one or more columns.

sketch-what-is-order-by.title: What is ORDER BY?
sketch-what-is-order-by.bodyMarkdown: The `ORDER BY` clause in SQL is used to sort the result set of a query by one or more columns. You can sort in ascending (`ASC`, the default) or descending (`DESC`) order. This helps you organize your data in a meaningful way, such as listing products by price or by name alphabetically.

sketch-basic-example.title: Basic Example
sketch-basic-example.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you want to see all products sorted by price from lowest to highest:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products
ORDER BY price ASC;
```

To sort by price from highest to lowest, use `DESC`:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC;
```

sketch-multi-column-order.title: Sorting by Multiple Columns
sketch-multi-column-order.bodyMarkdown:
You can sort by more than one column. For example, to sort products by category (A-Z), and then by price (low to high) within each category:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products
ORDER BY category ASC, price ASC;
```

This is useful when you want to group and then order items within those groups.

sketch-order-by-and-null.title: ORDER BY and NULL Values
sketch-order-by-and-null.bodyMarkdown: When sorting, SQL places `NULL` values either at the beginning or end, depending on the database and sort order. In SQLite, `NULL` values come first in ascending order and last in descending order. In our `products` table, all columns are NOT NULL, so this is not an issue here.

quiz-single-choice-basic-order.title: Sorting Products by Price
quiz-single-choice-basic-order.prompt: Which SQL clause will sort the products from the lowest to the highest price?
quiz-single-choice-basic-order.hint: Use ORDER BY with the column and ASC for ascending order.

help.concept: The ORDER BY clause sorts results by the correct answer specified column. ASC means ascending order.
help.hint_1: The correct clause uses ORDER BY, the column name, and ASC.
help.hint_2: Remember, ASC is the default, but writing it makes your intent clear.

quiz-multi-choice-multi-column.title: Sorting by Multiple Columns
quiz-multi-choice-multi-column.prompt: Which of the following queries will sort products by category (A-Z), then by price (low to high) within each category? Select all that apply.
quiz-multi-choice-multi-column.hint: Pay attention to the order of columns and sort directions.

help.concept: When sorting by multiple columns, the order in ORDER BY matters. ASC is default.
help.hint_1: category must come before price in ORDER BY for the grouping to work as described.
help.hint_2: Both explicit ASC and omitting ASC (default) will sort ascending.

options.a: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY category ASC, price ASC;
options.b: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC, category ASC;
options.c: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY category, price;
options.d: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY category DESC, price ASC;

quiz-drag-reorder-output.title: Result Order by Price DESC
quiz-drag-reorder-output.prompt:
Drag the product names to show the order of results when running:

```sql
SELECT name FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;
```
quiz-drag-reorder-output.hint: Start with the highest price and go down.

help.concept: ORDER BY price DESC sorts from highest to lowest price.
help.hint_1: Look for the product with the largest price first.
help.hint_2: Arrange all products from most expensive to least expensive.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.title: ORDER BY Keyword
quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete the SQL statement for sorting products by stock (highest first):

```sql
SELECT * FROM products _____ stock DESC;
```
quiz-fill-blank-choice-keyword.hint: Which clause is used for sorting?

help.concept: the missing term is the SQL clause for sorting results.
help.hint_1: It's not GROUP BY or SORT BY.

quiz-code-input-basic.title: Write a Query: Sort by Name
quiz-code-input-basic.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the products table, sorted alphabetically by product name (A-Z).
quiz-code-input-basic.hint: Use ORDER BY with the name column.

help.concept: ORDER BY lets you sort results by any column, such as name.
help.hint_1: The column to sort by is name. ASC is the default for alphabetical order.
help.hint_2: ORDER BY name will sort A-Z.
